summary: Rules apply default-deny access, verified bootstrap ownership, immutable client ownership, permission-scoped admin access, strict booking-message schemas, validated state transitions, and bounded payloads. The remaining risks are low and relate to intentionally public business settings and legacy-compatible booking documents.

findings.issue: settings/pricing and settings/invoice are publicly readable so the unauthenticated client landing page can display prices and studio contact details.
findings.recommendation: Keep these documents free of secrets and move any future private settings to a separate admin-only document.

findings.issue: Booking writes validate all security-sensitive fields but accept additional legacy business fields used by billing and scheduling.
findings.recommendation: After legacy booking data is normalized, add a hasOnly field allowlist and emulator tests for every booking workflow.
